title: Ephemerantha simplicicaulis
intro: Ephemerantha simplicicaulis, also known as the "Yellow Ginger Lily," is a species of flowering plant in the family Zingiberaceae. The plant is traditionally valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits that include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in relieving pain and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Ephemerantha simplicicaulis include rhizome extracts rich in gingerols, shogaols, and other compounds with potential therapeutic applications. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include dried and powdered rhizome, as well as extracts used in traditional remedies and modern supplements.
taxonomy: Ephemerantha simplicicaulis is classified under the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the order Asparagales. The genus Ephemerantha is often considered a synonym of the genus Paphiopedilum. However, some sources still recognize Ephemerantha simplicicaulis as a distinct species. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and current taxonomic research.
